children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: After receiving input from interested individuals with relevant expertise, the Secretary shall develop and implement a comprehensive plan for activities carried out under parts B and C in order to enhance the provision of early intervention services, educational services, related services, and transitional services to children with disabilities under subchapters II and III. To the extent practicable, the plan shall be coordinated with the plan developed pursuant to and shall include mechanisms to address early intervention, educational, related service and transitional needs identified by State educational agencies in applications submitted for State personnel development grants under part A and for grants under parts B and C.section 9567b(c) of this title1See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Public comment
children.label: 2 Public comment
children.text: The Secretary shall provide a public comment period of not less than 45 days on the plan.

children.heading: Distribution of funds
children.label: 3 Distribution of funds
children.text: In implementing the plan, the Secretary shall, to the extent appropriate, ensure that funds awarded under parts B and C are used to carry out activities that benefit, directly or indirectly, children with the full range of disabilities and of all ages.

children.heading: Reports to Congress
children.label: 4 Reports to Congress
children.text: The Secretary shall annually report to Congress on the Secretary’s activities under parts B and C, including an initial report not later than 12 months after .December 3, 2004

children.heading: Assistance authorized
children.label: b Assistance authorized
children.text: The Secretary is authorized to award grants to, or enter into contracts or cooperative agreements with, eligible entities to enable the eligible entities to carry out the purposes of such parts in accordance with the comprehensive plan described in subsection (a).

children.heading: Application requirement
children.label: 1 Application requirement
children.text: In making an award of a grant, contract, or cooperative agreement under part B or C, the Secretary shall, as appropriate, require an eligible entity to demonstrate how the eligible entity will address the needs of children with disabilities from minority backgrounds.

children.label: A
children.text: Providing outreach and technical assistance to historically Black colleges and universities, and to institutions of higher education with minority enrollments of not less than 25 percent, to promote the participation of such colleges, universities, and institutions in activities under this part.

children.label: B
children.text: Enabling historically Black colleges and universities, and the institutions described in subparagraph (A), to assist other colleges, universities, institutions, and agencies in improving educational and transitional results for children with disabilities, if the historically Black colleges and universities and the institutions of higher education described in subparagraph (A) meet the criteria established by the Secretary under this part.

children.label: A
children.text: age ranges;

children.label: B
children.text: disabilities;

children.label: C
children.text: school grades;

children.label: D
children.text: types of educational placements or early intervention environments;

children.label: E
children.text: types of services;

children.label: F
children.text: content areas, such as reading; or

children.label: G
children.text: effective strategies for helping children with disabilities learn appropriate behavior in the school and other community based educational settings;
projects that address the needs of children based on the severity or incidence of their disability;

children.label: A
children.text: low achieving students;

children.label: B
children.text: underserved populations;

children.label: C
children.text: children from low income families;

children.label: D
children.text: limited English proficient children;

children.label: E
children.text: unserved and underserved areas;

children.label: F
children.text: rural or urban areas;

children.label: G
children.text: children whose behavior interferes with their learning and socialization;

children.label: H
children.text: children with reading difficulties;

children.label: I
children.text: children in public charter schools;

children.label: J
children.text: children who are gifted and talented; or

children.label: K
children.text: children with disabilities served by local educational agencies that receive payments under title VII of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 [ et seq.];20 U.S.C. 7701
projects to reduce inappropriate identification of children as children with disabilities, particularly among minority children;
projects that are carried out in particular areas of the country, to ensure broad geographic coverage;

projects that promote the development and use of technologies with universal design, assistive technology devices, and assistive technology services to maximize children with disabilities’ access to and participation in the general education curriculum; and
any activity that is authorized in part B or C.

children.heading: Eligibility for financial assistance
children.label: e Eligibility for financial assistance
children.text: No State or local educational agency, or other public institution or agency, may receive a grant or enter into a contract or cooperative agreement under part B or C that relates exclusively to programs, projects, and activities pertaining to children aged 3 through 5, inclusive, unless the State is eligible to receive a grant under .section 1419(b) of this title
